CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(100));
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id > 0;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM temp_table WHERE name LIKE 'A%');
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'Charlie' WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM temp_table WHERE name = 'Bob');
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, value DECIMAL(10, 2));
CREATE TABLE combined_table AS SELECT a.id, a.name, b.value FROM test_table a JOIN another_table b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT * FROM combined_table WHERE value > (SELECT AVG(value) FROM another_table);
